results.title: Facebook Sued Over Mental Health Trauma to Content Moderators in Kenya | corpwatch
results.content: # Facebook Sued Over Mental Health Trauma to Content Moderators in Kenya. Facebook’s parent company has been sued in Kenya over mental health trauma suffered by Daniel Motaung, a former content moderator who worked for Sama, a contractor hired to remove graphic and violent content on the social media site. Motaung alleges that Facebook, Meta and Sama trafficked workers from around Africa to do dangerous work for minimal wages, failed to provide promised mental health counseling and refused to allow them to set up a union. "Daniel Motaung has sued @Meta and @SamaAI on his own behalf and on behalf of all CURRENT and FORMER content moderators. "We can’t have safe social media if the workers who protect us toil in a digital sweatshop,” said Cori Crider, director of Foxglove, in a statement. Motaung, who is from South Africa, says that he took the job at Sama under the impression that he was going to work at a call center because he could speak Zulu.
